triggerExamples: Build me a map of supplier risk.
Create a territory map for these accounts.
Show these locations on an interactive map artifact.
confidenceHints: The user asks for a map, geography, territory, route, supplier location, region, or spatial risk view.
The expected output is an interactive map or map-centered artifact.

inputs.label: Geography or territory scope
inputs.description: The regions, locations, addresses, entities, or territories to map.

inputs.label: Map data sources
inputs.description: Files, connectors, or user-provided data that contain location or territory fields.

confirmation.title: Build Map Artifact
confirmation.summary: Computer will discover location data, analyze spatial groupings or risks, produce an interactive map artifact, and validate that mapped points and regions match the evidence.
phaseSummary: Discover entities, location fields, regions, and source quality.
Analyze spatial groupings, risk signals, or territory patterns.
Validate mapped entities, caveats, and unsupported locations.

phases.title: Discover map data
taskSeeds: Identify location-bearing entities, fields, and source quality.
Capture unmappable, ambiguous, missing, or low-confidence locations.

phases.title: Analyze spatial patterns
taskSeeds: Derive useful groupings, layers, risk categories, and map controls.
Decide how to represent ambiguous or missing location evidence.

phases.title: Produce map artifact
taskSeeds: Generate an interactive map artifact with `MapView`, layers, filters, entity details, and proportional location caveats.
Persist the map artifact through `save_app` with map runbook metadata and the current thread id.

phases.title: Validate map result
taskSeeds: Verify mapped entities against source evidence.
Confirm caveats for missing, approximate, or inferred locations are visible.

outputs.title: Map artifact
outputs.description: A generated interactive map or map-centered dashboard artifact.

outputs.title: Location evidence and caveats
outputs.description: Notes about mapped, unmapped, ambiguous, and inferred locations.
